Description: Count affected references before deleting entities
Do: Count refs, show confirmation alert with count, define fallback
Don't: Silently delete entities referenced by other models
Code Good: let count = items.filter { $0.category == cat }.count; if count > 0 { showAlert(count) }
Code Bad: modelContext.delete(category) // no ref check

Description: Dismiss view BEFORE deleting SwiftData model
Do: dismiss() first, capture context+object, delete in Task @MainActor
Don't: Delete then dismiss — view renders tombstoned @Bindable → crash
Code Bad: modelContext.delete(item); dismiss() // crash

Description: #Predicate only works with primitive types
Do: Use Date/Decimal/Int/String in #Predicate, filter enum in-memory
Don't: Use enum .rawValue in #Predicate — compiles but crashes at runtime
Code Bad: @Query(filter: #Predicate<T> { $0.type.rawValue == "expense" }) // crash

Description: SwiftData stores Decimal as TEXT in SQLite
Don't: Use Decimal values in #Predicate for comparisons
Code Bad: @Query(filter: #Predicate<T> { $0.balance < 0 }) // string compare

Description: Bare Decimal(string:) returns nil on non-US locales
Don't: Decimal(string: text) without locale
Code Bad: Decimal(string: input) // nil on EU locales

Description: Returns 0 for repeating decimals (Foundation bug)
Don't: NSDecimalNumber(...).intValue
Code Bad: NSDecimalNumber(decimal: result).intValue // returns 0

Description: New .swift files must be registered in Xcode project
Do: Add PBXBuildFile + PBXFileReference + Group + Sources phase
Don't: Assume Xcode auto-discovers CLI-created files
Code Good: // Add to project.pbxproj: PBXBuildFile, PBXFileReference, group, Sources
Code Bad: // Just create file and hope Xcode finds it

Description: Notifications: remove stale inside callback then add new
Do: getPendingNotificationRequests { remove stale → add new INSIDE }
Don't: Remove outside + add outside (race condition)
Code Good: center.getPendingNotificationRequests { reqs in let stale = reqs.filter {...}; center.removePending(stale); addNew() }
Code Bad: center.removePending(ids); center.add(new) // race

Description: .count only catches insert/delete not property mutations
Don't: onChange(of: items.count) for property changes
Code Bad: .onChange(of: items.count) // misses edits

Description: Always check available simulators before xcodebuild
Do: xcrun simctl list devices available | grep -i iphone
Don't: Hardcode simulator name (may not exist on machine)
Code Good: xcrun simctl list devices available | grep -i iphone | head -1
Code Bad: xcodebuild -destination 'name=iPhone 16' // may not exist

Description: Use startOfMonth(-N) to startOfCurrentMonth for historical data
Do: Exclude partial current month from averages
Don't: Include Date() as end date — inflates averages
Code Bad: fetch from: startOfMonth(-N) to: Date() // partial month

Description: Pass state as let values and mutations as closures
Do: let + () -> Void closures, mutations stay in parent
Don't: Pass viewModel/EnvironmentObject to sub-views
Code Good: struct SubView: View { let amount: Decimal; let onTap: () -> Void }
Code Bad: struct SubView: View { @ObservedObject var vm: ViewModel } // tight coupling

Description: Private members must widen to internal when splitting across files
Don't: Keep private on members used across files in same target
Code Bad: private func helper() { ... } // invisible from other file

Description: Keep views under 200 LOC where possible
Do: Split into parent orchestrator + sub-view files, target <200
Don't: Leave monolithic files >400 LOC
Code Bad: NetWorthView.swift (416 LOC) // all-in-one

Description: Use @State only for view-local transient state
Don't: @State for shared or persistent data
Code Bad: @State var transactions: [Transaction] // should be @Query

Description: Child views receive @Binding from parent @State
Don't: @Binding for data that doesn't come from parent @State
Code Good: struct Toggle: View { @Binding var isOn: Bool }
Code Bad: struct Toggle: View { @State var isOn: Bool } // disconnected from parent

Description: Use @StateObject for objects this view creates and owns
Don't: @StateObject for injected objects
Code Bad: @ObservedObject var vm = ViewModel() // recreated on re-render

Description: Prefer @Observable macro over ObservableObject for iOS 17+
Don't: Mix ObservableObject and @Observable in same target
Code Bad: class VM: ObservableObject { @Published var items: [Item] = [] } // legacy

Description: Use NavigationStack (iOS 16+) not deprecated NavigationView
Don't: NavigationView (deprecated iOS 16+)
Code Bad: NavigationView { NavigationLink(destination: DetailView()) { ... } }

Description: Use LazyVStack/LazyHStack for 100+ items
Don't: VStack for large collections (loads all at once)
Code Bad: ScrollView { VStack { ForEach(largeList) { row in ... } } } // all loaded

Description: GeometryReader causes extra layout passes
Do: Use only when you truly need parent dimensions
Don't: GeometryReader for simple layouts, or nested
Code Bad: GeometryReader { geo in Text("Hello").frame(width: geo.size.width) } // overkill

Description: Modifier order affects visual output — background before padding differs
Do: Apply modifiers in logical visual order
Don't: Random modifier ordering
Code Bad: Text("Hi").background(.blue).padding() // blue only behind text

Description: Use .task modifier for lifecycle-aware async work
Don't: .onAppear { Task { await fetchData() } } — no auto-cancel
Code Bad: List { ... }.onAppear { Task { data = await api.fetch() } } // no cancel

Description: Add Equatable conformance to reduce unnecessary re-renders
Don't: Expensive computations in body without memoization
Code Good: struct Row: View, Equatable { let item: Item; static func == ... }
Code Bad: struct Row: View { let item: Item } // re-renders on every parent change

Description: All interactive elements need accessibility labels
Don't: Images/buttons without accessibility context
Code Bad: Button { delete() } label: { Image(systemName: "trash") } // silent to VoiceOver

Description: Support Dynamic Type for all text
Do: Use system fonts or .font(.body), test with large text
Don't: Fixed font sizes that break on large Dynamic Type
Code Bad: Text("Price").font(.system(size: 14)) // breaks on large type

Description: Separate view logic into testable ViewModels
Don't: Business logic directly in View body
Code Good: class VM { func refresh() { items = service.fetch() } } // testable
Code Bad: var body: some View { let items = fetchItems() ... } // untestable

Description: All new views should have #Preview macros
Don't: Ship new views without preview macros
Code Bad: // no preview — hard to iterate

Description: Always pass custom categories when resolving categoryId
Don't: AppCategory.find(id) without custom: parameter
Code Bad: AppCategory.find(catId) // falls back to wrong category
